findings.description: This rule merges two distinct directives with opposite polarity: an affirmative list of known-safe color pairings (must/should use) and a prohibition on gold/sunshine text on white (must_not). Tagging the whole cluster polarity=must_not misrepresents the affirmative safe-pairs guidance and loses the exception noted for golds ('surfaces, rules, large display emphasis only').

new_rules.text: Use known-safe color pairs (Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy/Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy).
Do not use Gold or Sunshine text on White; golds are restricted to surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

findings.description: u_0003 ('Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning') is a distinct accessibility guideline not represented by any candidate rule; it has been dropped from extraction entirely.

payload.text: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

findings.description: Rule references WCAG AA standard and webaim.com as the required testing tool/authority but no governance facet (standard citation, required tool, verification method) is captured on the rule.
The absolute must_not polarity on the merged rule ignores the carve-out that gold/sunshine is permitted for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis — an unqualified prohibition misstates scope.
